item.title: Trump accuses Iran of "foolish violation" after ship struck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hezbollah says Israel must withdraw forces from Lebanon and Iran strikes a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as negotiations toward a peace deal falter.

item.title: How long do debt collectors have to collect from an estate?
item.description: Debt collectors have a limited time to make claims against an estate, so it's important to know that firm deadline.

item.title: Billionaire Leon Black testifying before House Epstein panel
item.description: An investor who employed and was close to Jeffrey Epstein is appearing before members of Congress investigating the deceased sexual abuser.

item.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk murder case finds prosecutor in contempt
item.description: The defense team for Tyler Robinson asked that the death penalty be taken off the table following public comments by prosecutors.

item.title: John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining classified information
item.description: Bolton agreed to pay a $2.25 million fine, pleading guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 28)
item.description: Jane Pauley hosts a special program celebrating our nation's semiquincentennial, "These United States - America at 250."

item.title: "Regime Change" authors on Trump's "information bubble" and use of power
item.description: In "Regime Change, Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump," Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan describe his fundamentally different approach to running the country.

item.title: Senate Democrats push for info on "anti-weaponization" fund, demand hearing
item.description: Led by Sen. Cory Booker of New Jersey, a group of Senate Democrats suggested the Justice Department violated its internal policies with the creation of the "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: John Bolton, former Trump adviser, pleads guilty to retaining classified information
item.description: Former Trump national security adviser John Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining classified government information. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more details.

item.title: Jalen Brunson's mom on teaching him to be a leader: "It's how you respond"
item.description: The Second Round Foundation, founded by Sandra and Jalen Brunson, aims to help provide young people with what they need to thrive in the future.

item.title: Go behind the scenes with Druski ahead of the BET Awards
item.description: Comedian and entertainer Druski will host the BET Awards on Sunday. At 31, he's the youngest host in the show's history. He gave "CBS Mornings" co-host Nate Burleson a preview of what to expect.

item.title: Newsom proposes federal billionaire tax and AI "public equity" fund
item.description: As California Gov. Gavin Newsom eyes a 2028 presidential bid, he's calling for a national tax on billionaires and a public stake in AI, though he opposes a state ballot measure to tax billionaires.

item.title: Sandra Brunson on helping son Jalen Brunson lead: "It's not what happens, it's how you respond"
item.description: Sandra Brunson, the mother of Knicks star Jalen Brunson, talks with "CBS Mornings" about how she helped her son become a leader and the nonprofit they formed together to help young people.

item.title: Have $45,000 worth of credit card debt? Here's how much you could get forgiven.
item.description: Stuck with tens of thousands in credit card debt? The right strategy could dramatically reduce your balance.

item.title: The cost of owning a home is soaring. Here's how to save money on your next project.
item.description: The price of buying a home is higher than ever, but the expenses don't stop there. Most homes will need repairs. Kelly O'Grady provides money-saving tips for your next project.

item.title: 4 people convicted over insecticide deaths of tourist family at hotel
item.description: Investigators initially suspected food poisoning, but that was quickly dismissed after Turkish media reported that their hotel was dealing with a bedbug infestation.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $40,000 long-term CD account can earn now
item.description: Long-term CD accounts offer extended financial protection and big returns for those looking to deposit $40,000 now.

item.title: Ukraine launches huge drone attack on Russia and occupied Crimea
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine will ramp up attacks on Russia in an operation "aimed at compelling it to end the war."

item.title: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan on how Trump's second presidency is a "regime change"
item.description: New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan join "CBS Mornings" to discuss their new book, "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump," and how President Trump's second term is a "fundamentally different conception of the U.S. presidency."

item.title: U.S. loses World Cup match to Turkey after resting some stars
item.description: The U.S. gave up the tiebreaking goal to Turkey in the 98th minute of the match on Thursday. Despite the loss, the U.S. still advances to the next round. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: How Sail 250 will celebrate America
item.description: Sail 250 is a parade of dozens of tall ships and naval vessels from more than 20 nations in honor of the United States' 250th birthday. The ships make their way from New Orleans to New York Harbor for the Fourth of July weekend. Rob Marciano has more.

item.title: Cassidy says Trump "was berating" senators during meeting: "I'm not there to be berated"
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that President Trump "was berating" the senators who supported the Iran war powers resolution during a meeting. "Frankly, I'm not there to be berated," Cassidy said. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: House GOP leadership cancels day of votes amid standoff over SAVE America Act
item.description: House Republican leaders canceled all votes planned for Friday amid the standoff over the SAVE America Act, a voting regulations bill. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: U.S. team suffers first 2026 World Cup loss, heads to knockout round
item.description: The U.S. men's team had already clinched its spot in the Round of 32, the knockout round, with its 2-0 win over Australia on Friday.

item.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll doubles
item.description: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed almost 600 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

item.title: Iran strikes vessel in Strait of Hormuz amid debate over "transit fees"
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a cargo ship near Oman on Thursday, further complicating the Iran-U.S. negotiations.

item.title: Internal emails show how RFK Jr.'s team sought to sway the CDC
item.description: A trove of emails offers a new look at how the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention navigated some of the most controversial decisions of President Trump's second term.

item.title: Jury remains deadlocked in trial of Palisades Fire arson suspect
item.description: A jury remains deadlocked in the trial of Jonathan Rinderknecht, the man accused of starting the blaze that turned into the deadly and destructive Palisades Fire in 2025.

item.title: Jalen Brunson's mother says she texts him encouragement before every game
item.description: Sandra Brunson, mother of New York Knicks superstar Jalen Brunson, says she's texted her son words of encouragement before every game since he was in high school. "I still send them, and he looks forward to them," Brunson says. See the full interview with Dr. Jon LaPook, Friday on "CBS Mornings."

item.title: Jalen Brunson's mom, Sandra, on what she told him about leadership
item.description: Sandra Brunson, the mother of NBA Finals MVP Jalen Brunson, says she told her son, "As a leader, he has to understand that everyone has a job to do and every one of his teammates has to feel comfortable in their role and believe in their role." See the full interview with Dr. Jon LaPook, Friday on "CBS Mornings."
